passages.title: The Blockbuster Phenomenon: a new museum trend
passages.source_html:
The Blockbuster Phenomenon: a new museum trend


Museums in Australia, like other pleasure-giving public organizations, are adapting their activities so that they more closely reflect the marketplace


A


Since the 1980s, the term 'blockbuster' has become the fashionable word for spectacular, high-profile museum exhibitions that have the ability to attract large crowds. A blockbuster is a 'large-scale loan exhibition that people who normally don't go to museums will stand in line for hours to see' (Elsen 1984). Once the museum that created the exhibition has shown it to their local market, it can be offered to other organizations for a fee. This means that you can boost your own door takings and make money from boosting someone else's door takings.


B


While partaking of the excitement of the blockbuster, visitors thus lured are likely to stay longer at the museum. Betty Churcher, the director of the Australian National Gallery, summed up the new blockbuster creed as follows: The bonus of the blockbuster exhibitions is that people come to see the blockbuster and they stay to look at the permanent collection, so you are getting broader exposure for your collection.


C


Museums across the UK, USA, Canada and Australia currently operate under a system of plural funding: revenue raised through contributions by federal, state and/or local governments, combined with revenue raised through admission charges and other activities. Maintaining and increasing visitor levels is thus paramount and involves not only creating or hiring blockbuster exhibitions, but providing regular exhibition changes and innovations. In addition, the visiting public have become known as customers rather than visitors, and the skills that are valued in museums to keep the new customers coming through the door have changed. Curators are now administrators and being a museum director no longer requires an arts degree – but public relations skills are essential if the museum is going to compete with other museums to stage traveling exhibition which draw huge crowds. 


D


The convergence of museums, the heritage industry, tourism, profit-making and pleasure-giving has resulted in the new 'museology'. This has given rise to much debate about whether it is appropriate to see museums primarily as tourist attractions. In literature from both UK and USA, the words that are starting to appear in some descriptions of blockbusters are 'less scholarly', 'non-elitist' and 'populist', while others extol the virtues encouraging scholars to co-operate on projects, and to provide exhibitions that cater for a broad selection of community rather than an elite sector.Whatever commentators may think, managers of museums worldwide are looking for artful ways to blend culture and commerce, and blockbuster exhibitions are at the top of the list. 


E


But do blockbusters held in public institutions really create a surplus to fund other activities? If the bottom line is profit, then according to the records of many major museums blockbusters do make money. For museums in some countries, it may be the money that they require to replace parts of their collections or to fix buildings that are in need of attention. For some museums in Australia, it may be the opportunity to illustrate that they are attempting to pay their way by recovering part of their operating costs. Also creating or hiring a blockbuster has many positive spin-offs: blockbusters mean crowds, and crowds are good for the local economy, providing increased trade for shops, hotels, restaurants, the transport industry and retailers. The argument that the arts provide sustained economic benefits has been well illustrated in impact studies in the USA and UK.


F


However, blockbusters require large capital expenditure, and draw on resources across all branches of an organization, and the costs don't end there. There is a Human Resource Management cost in addition to a measurable 'real' dollar cost. Receiving a touring exhibition draws resources from across functional management structures in project management style. Everyone, from general labourers to building services, front of house, technical, promotional, educational and administrative staff, is required to perform additional tasks. Furthermore, as an increasing number of institutions try their hand at increasing visitor numbers and memberships (and therefore revenue) by staging blockbuster exhibitions, it may be less likely that blockbusters will continue to provide a surplus to subsidize other activities due to the competitive nature of the market.


G


It has been illustrated in both the UK and USA that the blockbuster ideology has resulted in the false expectation that the momentum required to stage blockbusters can be maintained continually. Creating, mounting or hiring blockbusters is exhausting, with the real costs throughout an institution difficult to calculate. Secondly, as some analysts have argued, the 'shop keeping' mentality and cost-benefit analysis and a pure concentration on the bottom line, can squeeze substance out of an exhibition. Taking out substance can be a recipe for blockbuster failure and therefore financial failure. 


H


Perhaps the best pathway to take is one that balances both blockbusters and regular exhibitions. However, this easy middle ground may only work if you have enough space, and have alternate sources of funding to continue to support the regular, less exciting fare. Perhaps the advice should be to make sure that you find out what your local community, wants from you and make sure that your regular activities and exhibitions are more enticing.

groups.title: Questions 1-13
groups.source_html:
Questions 1 - 4


Look at the eight paragraphs (A - H). Which paragraph contains the following information?

Choose the correct letter (A - H). You may choose any letter more than once.

Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
1

1 the reason why museum directors need to constantly alter and update their exhibits
✅ 正确答案：C

定位词: reason, alter and update


对应原文: C段, Maintaining and increasing visitor levels is thus paramount and involves not only creating or hiring blockbuster exhibitions, but providing regular exhibition changes and innovations.


详细解析:C段指出博物馆为了保持和增加参观人数，不仅需要举办大型展览，还需要定期更换和创新展览内容。这正是博物馆馆长需要不断改变和更新展览的原因。


同义替换：constantly=regular；alter and update =changes and innovations
2

2 mention of the length of time people will queue up to see a blockbuster
✅ 正确答案：A

定位词: length of time,queue up


对应原文: A段, A blockbuster is a 'large-scale loan exhibition that people who normally don't go to museums will stand in line for hours to see' (Elsen 1984).


详细解析:A段指出“大型展览是指那些连平时不去博物馆的人都会排队数小时观看的展览”，明确说明了观众排队的时长。


同义替换:queue up=stand in line , Length of time=hours
3

3 terms that people have used when referring to blockbusters 
✅ 正确答案：D

定位词: terms , blockbusters


对应原文:D段, In literature from both UK and USA, the words that are starting to appear in some descriptions of blockbusters are 'less scholarly', 'non-elitist' and 'populist', while others extol the virtues encouraging scholars to co-operate on projects, and to provide exhibitions that cater for a broad selection of community rather than an elite sector. 


详细解析:D段指出在英国和美国的文献中，一些词语如“学术性较弱”、“非精英化”和“平民化”被用来描述大型展览。这些词语是指人们用来形容大型展览的术语。


同义替换:Terms=words, Referring to blockbusters=descriptions of blockbusters
4

4 the various ways that institutions like museums get financial support
✅ 正确答案：C

定位词: financial support


对应原文: C段, Museums across the UK, USA, Canada and Australia currently operate under a system of plural funding: revenue raised through contributions by federal, state and/or local governments, combined with revenue raised through admission charges and other activities.


详细解析:C段指出博物馆通过“联邦、州或地方政府的资助，以及门票收入和其他活动”获取资金支持。


同义替换: financial support=revenue raised

Questions 5 - 8


Complete the sentences. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS in each gap.

5

These days, museum visitors tend to be referred to as .

正确答案： 5 customers
Question 5

定位词: museum visitors, referred to as


对应原文: C段, In addition, the visiting public have become known as customers rather than visitors, and the skills that are valued in museums to keep the new customers coming through the door have changed.


详细解析:C段指出公众现在被称为“客户”，而不是传统意义上的“参观者”。

6

Museum curators now need  rather than academic qualifications.

正确答案： 6 public relations skills
Question 6

定位词: curators,academic qualifications


对应原文: C段, Curators are now administrators and being a museum director no longer requires an arts degree – but public relations skills are essential if the museum is going to compete with other museums to stage traveling exhibition which draw huge crowds. 


详细解析:C段指出，现在的博物馆馆长需要的是“公关技能”，而不是艺术学位或其他学术资格。


同义替换：academic qualifications = arts degree

7

The linking of a range of public institutions that entertain the public is known as.

正确答案： 7 the new museology##the new 'museology'
Question 7

定位词: linking, public institutions


对应原文: D段, The convergence of museums, the heritage industry, tourism, profit-making and pleasure-giving has resulted in the new 'museology'.


详细解析:D段指出博物馆、遗产产业、旅游业等的结合（融合）被称为“新博物馆学”。


同义替换:Linking=convergence, entertain=pleasure-giving

8

There is discussion about whether museums can be regarded in the same way as other .

正确答案： 8 tourist attractions
Question 8

定位词: discussion, the same way


对应原文: D段，This has given rise to much debate about whether it is appropriate to see museums primarily as tourist attractions.


详细解析:D段指出，博物馆是否可以与其他旅游景点相提并论的问题正在引发讨论。


同义替换: Discussion=debate


Questions 9 - 10


Choose TWO correct answers.

Options (Word Bank):
A. Some of the money they raise can be used for structural repairs.
B. They can provide funds to help support amateur artists.
C. Local services benefit from the extra business they bring about.
D. They encourage overseas workers into the local area.
E. They raise employee performance levels.
9

Which TWO of the following are mentioned by the writer as advantages of blockbusters?

正确答案： 1 A. Some of the money they raise can be used for structural repairs.  |  2 C. Local services benefit from the extra business they bring about.

定位词: advantages of blockbusters


对应原文：E段，For museums in some countries, it may be the money that they require to replace parts of their collections or to fix buildings that are in need of attention. 

Also creating or hiring a blockbuster has many positive spin-offs: blockbusters mean crowds, and crowds are good for the local economy, provide increased trade for shops, hotels, restaurants, the transport industry and retailers. 


详细解析:E段指出，热门展览的可以为博物馆的其他活动提供资金支持，例如更换收藏品或修理建筑物。另外，热门展览会导致当地的商店、酒店、餐馆、交通运输业和零售商的生意会增加。


同义替换: structural repairs=fix buildings；extra business=increased trade

Questions 11 - 13


Choose THREE correct answers.


Options (Word Bank):
A. They do not suit museum management styles.
B. Specialist business advice has to be paid for.
C. They involve an increased workload for personnel.
D. They do not increase overall annual visitor numbers.
E. They are very tiring to put on.
F. What is popular in one country may not be popular in another.
G. The content can be weakened through financial pressure.
11

Which THREE of the following are mentioned by the writer as disadvantages of blockbusters?

正确答案： 1 C. They involve an increased workload for personnel.   |  2 E. They are very tiring to put on.  |  3 G. The content can be weakened through financial pressure.

定位词: disadvantages of blockbusters


对应段落: Paragraphs F and G


对应原文: 

F段 ，Everyone, from general labourers to building services, front of house, technical, promotional, educational and administrative staff, is required to perform additional tasks. 

G段，Creating, mounting or hiring blockbusters is exhausting, with the real costs throughout an institution difficult to calculate.

G段 ，Secondly, as some analysts have argued, the 'shop keeping' mentality and cost-benefit analysis and a pure concentration on the bottom line, can squeeze substance out of an exhibition.Taking out substance can be a recipe for blockbuster failure and therefore financial failure.


详细解析:

F段指出,从普通工人到管理人员都需要承担额外工作，与题干“增加员工工作量”一致。未提到管理风格不适应,没有提到需要支付商业咨询费用,没有提到无法增加年度总参观人数,未提及不同国家的受欢迎程度差异。G段指出, 举办大型展览的过程非常疲惫，与题干“耗费精力”对应。G段指出,过于注重经济效益可能削弱展览内容，导致失败，与题干中“内容可能因经济压力被削弱”一致。


同义替换: Increased workload= additional tasks; personnel=staff；tiring= exhausting; Content can be weakened= taking out substance
